prompts.prompt: Click on the landscape icon to change the document orientation.
Click on the align text
Click the 'X' button in the top right corner to close the application.
Click on the 'Layout' tab in the ribbon to access page setup options.
Click the 'References' tab to view the options for adding objects like tables and pictures.
Click the 'Pictures' button in the Illustrations group to insert an image
Right-click the 'Insert' tab to access options like 'Customize the Ribbon'.
Click the 'Table' button to open the grid for inserting a new table.
Right-click the 'Equation' button.
Right-click the 'Shapes' button in the Illustrations group to see options like adding it to the Quick Access Toolbar.
Click on 'Red Orange' in the open Colors dropdown menu to apply that color scheme.
Click the 'Page Borders' in the Page Background.
Click the 'Watermark' button in the Page Background group to add a watermark to the document.
Click the 'Themes' button on the far left of the Design ribbon to browse overall document themes.
Double-click the top title bar where it says 'Document1 - Word' to toggle the window size.
Right-click the 'Page Borders' button on the ribbon to access the option to add it to the Quick Access Toolbar.
Type 'change theme colors' into the Search bar at the top center of the application window.
Click the size option to open the dropdown and increase the size
Type 'replace text' into the Search box located at the top center of the application window.
Click the 'Paste' option in the Clipboard group on the left side of the ribbon.
Click the 'B' (Bold) icon in the Font group on the ribbon to format the currently active text.
Click the 'Shape Fill' dropdown in the Shape Styles group to change the background color of the selected text box.
In the left section, change the number '01' in the text box to '9'
Click the 'Alt Text' button in the Accessibility group on the ribbon to add a description to the shape.
Click the up arrow next to the height measurement ('1.88 cm') in the Size group to increase the height of the shape.
Double-click the text in the '02' section to select it
Right-click the 'Shape Effects' button in the Shape Styles group to view ribbon customization options.
Type '5 cm' into the width measurement box in the Size group (currently displaying '4.65 cm').
Click on 'Heading 5' within the expanded Styles gallery to apply it to the currently selected text.
Type 'Important: ' directly into the document to replace the sentence "Table of Contents".
Click on 'Subtitle' in the Styles menu to apply the subtitle style to the selected text.
Click the 'Center' alignment button in the Paragraph group on the ribbon to center the selected text.
Double-click the text 'Heading 2' to select it.
Double-click the 'Format Painter' icon (the paintbrush) in the Clipboard group on the far left of the ribbon to lock it on.
Right-click the highlighted text "Heading 2" to open the mini toolbar and text context menu.
Right-click the 'Heading 5' style block in the open gallery to access options like 'Modify...' or 'Update to Match Selection'.
Type 'update table of contents' into the Search box located at the top center of the application window.
